contrastMetadata.transferBoundary: Re-evaluate the strategy whenever signs, query volume, update frequency, objective semantics, required outputs, or retained-state limits change; name the exact property that preserves or destroys the discard rule.

items.prompt: A minimum-length sum-at-least-k task changes from strictly positive values to arbitrary signed values. What should happen to the strategy?

options.text: Abandon the simple monotone window proof and consider prefix sums with an order-aware structure.
Keep the same shrink-when-valid rule unchanged.
Sort values before moving boundaries.
Use only a frequency map for exact differences.

feedback.reason: Changing the sign domain destroys the former discard invariant.
feedback.details: The strategy must transfer with the changed proof obligation, not with the familiar problem title.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.same_window: Negative values can reverse both expansion and shrink effects.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.prefix_hash_count: The objective is shortest threshold, not exact count.

items.prompt: A task changes from one fixed interval sum to millions of arbitrary static interval sums. Which transfer is natural?

options.text: Pay one prefix build and reuse constant-time range differences.
Rescan every interval regardless of workload.
Use one current window even though queries jump arbitrarily.
Sort once to preserve original interval sums.

feedback.reason: Query volume and boundary order now justify reusable cumulative state.
feedback.details: The same arithmetic result has a different best representation under the new workload.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.keep_one_scan: Repeated work can grow to O(qn).
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.toward_window: A moving state does not provide random access.

items.prompt: A count-all-exact-sum-k task changes to minimum-length sum-at-least-k on positive values. Which transfer is appropriate?

options.text: Replace prefix-frequency counting with a monotone threshold window.
A prefix frequency map directly returns the minimum threshold length.
Sort and use opposite-end pointers.
Result semantics never affect retained state.

feedback.reason: The objective changes from all exact matches to one optimized threshold interval.
feedback.details: Positive values supply the directional boundary rule needed by the moving window.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.keep_frequency: It counts exact differences and lacks the needed optimization order.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.same_payload: Count and minimum length require different state.

items.prompt: A static range-query system starts accepting frequent point updates. What changes?

options.text: A plain prefix array may require costly rebuilds; a dynamic cumulative structure or different workload strategy is needed.
Stored prefixes remain correct after arbitrary updates.
A single sliding window now answers every dynamic arbitrary range.
Only query count belongs in the model.

feedback.reason: Static preprocessing is tied to one source version.
feedback.details: A transfer decision must include update maintenance or rebuild cost.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.window_all_queries: Random ranges still need a suitable index.

items.prompt: A workload changes from arbitrary range queries to one left-to-right enumeration of every fixed-size block. Which transfer can reduce space?

options.text: Replace the full prefix array with a rolling aggregate if no later random queries remain.
Prefix state is mandatory whenever more than one interval is examined.
Sort every block.
Store every cumulative sum in a hash map.

feedback.reason: The access pattern changed from random reusable queries to one sequential pass.
feedback.details: A moving sum retains only the state that the new workload consumes.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.keep_prefix_required: Sequential overlap can be reused with constant state.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.sort_blocks: Order is irrelevant and sorting is wasteful.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.hash_prefix: No historical exact-match lookup is required.

items.prompt: A batch range-sum service changes into an online last-k stream metric. Which property drives the transfer?

options.text: Answers are required at one evolving frontier before future data exists.
The output still uses sums, so the strategy cannot change.
Every repeated answer requires prefix storage.
Online processing implies values are negative.

feedback.reason: The state lifetime narrows from all historical interval boundaries to one current recent interval.
feedback.details: A rolling sum plus outgoing-value state can answer immediately.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.sum_keyword: The access pattern and timing changed.

items.prompt: An exact-sum existence task changes to counting every exact-sum interval. What prefix-hash change is required?

options.text: Replace boolean presence with occurrence frequencies.
Keep only the earliest index.
Retain only the current prefix.
Use one left pointer and discard old prefixes.

feedback.reason: The lookup key remains currentPrefix - k, but the payload must represent multiplicity.
feedback.details: Transfer can occur within the prefix family when the output contract changes.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.frequency_to_earliest: That supports longest length, not total count.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.move_left: That loses valid matches with mixed signs.

items.prompt: An O(n)-space prefix representation no longer fits the memory budget, and only one sequential fixed-window objective remains. What transfer is justified?

options.text: Use a rolling aggregate with constant auxiliary state.
Keep the prefix array because faster queries override a hard cap.
Recompute every window from scratch in O(nk) without considering overlap.
Sort to reduce memory.

feedback.reason: The workload no longer needs arbitrary interval access.
feedback.details: A resource constraint can therefore reverse the representation choice without changing the numeric objective.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.ignore_budget: The representation is not legal under the resource contract.

items.prompt: A product needs both immediate last-k stream metrics and later arbitrary historical range sums. What is the sound conclusion?

options.text: One moving state serves the live metric, while retained prefix or another historical index may serve later queries; include both costs.
The current rolling sum answers every historical interval.
Offline prefix construction can delay all live outputs.
Pick one method because the feature is called analytics.

feedback.reason: Mixed workloads can justify more than one representation.
feedback.details: The design must count retention, update, and synchronization costs rather than force one label.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.one_window_all: It has discarded earlier boundary state.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.choose_by_name: The workload contains two distinct relations.

items.prompt: After a requirement change, which resulting workloads make one evolving interval the useful state? Select all that apply.

options.text: Queries become one sequential traversal of overlapping intervals.
Answers are required at the current stream frontier.
The objective becomes a threshold under nonnegative values.
The workload becomes many arbitrary static range queries.

feedback.reason: Sliding-window transfers introduce one evolving interval and a legal directional update.
feedback.details: Random static ranges require broader retained boundary state.

items.prompt: After a requirement change, which resulting workloads require retained cumulative history? Select all that apply.

options.text: One query becomes many arbitrary static interval queries.
Values become arbitrary signed for an exact-sum objective.
The result changes from one match to counting all exact matches.
Only the current last-k stream interval remains relevant.

feedback.reason: Prefix transfers require cumulative history for random access or exact-boundary matching.
feedback.details: A single frontier-local interval needs less retained state.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.negative_exact: Historical prefix lookup avoids monotonicity assumptions.

items.prompt: Which changes can require a hybrid or third structure rather than a simple window or plain prefix array? Select all that apply.

options.text: Frequent point updates plus arbitrary range queries.
Shortest sum-at-least interval with arbitrary signed values.
Immediate rolling metrics plus later random historical queries.
One fixed-size sequential scan only.

feedback.reason: The contrast is not a forced binary choice when the contract requires capabilities neither simple representation supplies alone.
feedback.details: The transfer explanation should name the missing operation.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.one_fixed_scan: A simple rolling aggregate is sufficient.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.updates_ranges: A Fenwick tree or segment tree may be more appropriate.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.negative_shortest_threshold: Prefix sums plus a monotonic deque are needed.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.online_and_history: Two synchronized representations may be justified.

items.prompt: Which controlled changes test real transfer? Select all that apply.

options.text: Keep the objective but change positive values to arbitrary signed values.
Keep interval sums but change one query to many random queries.
Keep positive data but change exact count to minimum threshold length.
Rename the function without changing inputs, outputs, or constraints.

feedback.reason: Transfer is demonstrated when one material property changes and the strategy is re-derived.
feedback.details: Cosmetic wording should not affect classification.

items.prompt: Which explanations are required for a transferable strategy choice? Select all that apply.

options.text: Name the exact sign, objective, access-pattern, or update property that decides.
Explain the safe discard rule or cumulative lookup relation.
State what change would make the competing strategy appropriate.
Rely on the words contiguous sum.

feedback.reason: A transferable explanation exposes mechanism and counterfactual boundary.
feedback.details: Pattern names without conditions are weak evidence.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.discard_or_lookup: The state transition needs a proof.

items.prompt: Arrange the general transfer process.

elements.text: Identify the changed sign, objective, query volume, update rate, timing, or memory contract.
Re-derive the window discard rule and prefix lookup or query relation.
Recompute full time, peak space, preprocessing, and invalidation cost.
Keep, replace, or combine strategies and state the reversal condition.

feedback.reason: Transfer is a delta analysis over the previous decision.
feedback.details: A familiar surface form cannot preserve a strategy whose proof has disappeared.

items.prompt: Arrange the transfer after negative values are introduced into a positive threshold problem.

elements.text: Remove the claim that expansion only increases and shrinking only decreases the sum.
Show a negative incoming or outgoing value reversing the aggregate direction.
Determine whether the target is exact, threshold, count, shortest, or longest.
Choose prefix hash, monotonic deque, or another correct structure for that objective.

feedback.reason: The lost sign invariant is the reason for transfer.
feedback.details: The replacement structure depends on the exact result contract.

items.prompt: Arrange the transfer from one range query to many static range queries.

elements.text: Measure the one-query or cumulative direct scan work.
Confirm one immutable source version will serve the queries.
Construct cumulative state once when reuse justifies it.
Answer later ranges through prefix differences until invalidation.

feedback.reason: Preprocessing transfer requires both enough reuse and a stable source.
feedback.details: Without version validity, the cumulative representation cannot remain authoritative.

items.prompt: Arrange the transfer from exact-sum counting to a positive minimum-threshold objective.

elements.text: Recognize that frequencies of all matching prefixes no longer define the result.
Use positivity to derive monotone aggregate movement.
Treat sum at least target as a window validity condition.
Shrink while valid and record minimum lengths.

feedback.reason: The result changes from multiplicity of exact boundaries to optimization over a validity region.
feedback.details: That change permits a smaller moving state under positive values.

items.prompt: One interval of length m is summed directly, or a prefix array of n values is built and used for that one query. Select both times and the prefix auxiliary space.

feedback.reason: For one short interval, direct work can be lower than global preprocessing.
feedback.details: The conclusion reverses when enough compatible queries reuse the prefix array.

items.prompt: q arbitrary range sums over static n-value data are answered either by full O(n) scans or by one prefix build. Select both workload times.
constraints: Each direct query scans Θ(n) values in the worst case.

feedback.reason: Increasing compatible query volume creates the prefix-preprocessing transfer.
feedback.details: This comparison assumes the data remains unchanged.

items.prompt: Compute every fixed-size window sum over n values by rolling state or by a full prefix array. Select both times and auxiliary spaces.
constraints: Indexed input exposes outgoing values directly.

feedback.reason: Both are time-optimal for this sequential workload.
feedback.details: The transfer toward rolling state is a space decision unless future random queries need the prefixes.

items.prompt: A plain prefix array is rebuilt after each of u updates and then serves q total range queries. Select total time and peak prefix space.
constraints: Only the current prefix version remains live.

feedback.reason: Update frequency can eliminate the advantage of a static prefix representation.
feedback.details: A dynamic cumulative structure may be the correct transfer when both updates and queries are substantial.

contrastSets.transferBoundary: Re-evaluate the strategy whenever signs, query volume, update frequency, objective semantics, required outputs, or retained-state limits change; name the exact property that preserves or destroys the discard rule.
